headlines.headline: King Charles Addresses US Congress in Historic State Visit
headlines.summary: King Charles III became only the second British monarch to address a joint session of Congress, meeting with President Trump at the White House amid tensions over the Iran war. The state visit celebrates America's 250th anniversary of independence while navigating diplomatic strains.

headlines.headline: US Soldier Charged with Using Classified Intel to Win K on Polymarket
headlines.summary: A US special forces soldier pleaded not guilty to charges of using classified information about the mission to capture Venezuela's Maduro to win over ,000 on prediction market Polymarket. The case raises concerns about insider trading on prediction platforms.

headlines.headline: DOJ Indicts Former FBI Director James Comey a Second Time
headlines.summary: The US Department of Justice has indicted former FBI Director James Comey for a second time, according to CNN reports. The indictment marks an escalation in tensions between the Trump administration and the former FBI chief.

headlines.headline: UAE Announces Departure from OPEC, Dealing Major Blow to Oil Cartel
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates announced it will leave OPEC effective May 1, stripping the organization of its third-largest producer. The move comes amid tensions over production policies during the Iran conflict and could reshape global oil markets.

perspectives.angle: UAE Announces Exit from OPEC and OPEC+
perspectives.summary: The United Arab Emirates will leave the oil cartel OPEC and its OPEC+ alliance effective May 1, dealing a major blow to Saudi-led oil coordination during the Iran war energy crisis. The move ends nearly 60 years of UAE membership.

headlines.headline: Google Signs Classified AI Deal With Pentagon for Military Applications
headlines.summary: Alphabet's Google joined a growing list of technology firms signing deals with the U.S. Department of Defense to use AI models for classified work. The move marks a significant expansion of tech-military collaboration amid the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Gulf Leaders Hold Emergency Meeting in Saudi Arabia on Iran War Crisis
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia hosted an emergency consultative meeting with other Persian Gulf leaders in Jeddah to discuss the ongoing Iran conflict and its regional implications. The summit reflects growing coordination among Gulf states amid the Hormuz standoff.

headlines.headline: EU Trade Chief Resigns After Clashing Over Hasty Trump Deal
headlines.summary: Sabine Weyand, who led the European Commission's trade department for seven years, is leaving after clashing with superiors over a hastily agreed trade deal with U.S. President Donald Trump. The departure highlights tensions in EU-US trade relations.

headlines.headline: US Military Expands Secret Boat Strike Operations with More Aircraft
headlines.summary: The U.S. military has quietly increased the number of secret fixed-wing attack aircraft and armed MQ-9 Reaper drones for boat strike missions in the Iran conflict, according to officials. The expansion reflects the ongoing maritime dimension of the Hormuz standoff.

headlines.headline: Israel Rejects Russian Grain Theft Claims as Ukraine Threatens Sanctions
headlines.summary: Foreign Minister Saar accuses Zelensky of "Twitter diplomacy" after Ukrainian president claims Israel "cannot be unaware" that Russian grain cargo at Haifa port came from occupied territory.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine warns Israel of diplomatic crisis over ship carrying alleged stolen grain
perspectives.summary: Ukraine has warned of a potential diplomatic and legal crisis with Israel if the vessel Panormitis, carrying grain allegedly stolen from Russian-occupied Ukraine, docks at Haifa port.

headlines.headline: Indonesia Questions Lebanon Peacekeeping Mission as Casualties Mount
headlines.summary: Calls are growing in Indonesia to scale back or end UN peacekeeping contributions in Lebanon after casualties increased amid regional conflict escalation.

headlines.headline: France Demands Israel Remove All Gaza Aid Barriers
headlines.summary: France called on Israel to remove all restrictions on humanitarian aid to Gaza, citing dire conditions as the UN warns of worsening humanitarian crisis.

headlines.headline: Hong Kong Exports Surge 35.8% on AI Electronics Demand
headlines.summary: Hong Kong exports rose at the sharpest rate in over five years, up nearly 36% year-on-year in March, driven by strong demand for AI-related electronics.

headlines.headline: China Denounces Japan and EU Over South China Sea at UN
headlines.summary: China accused Japan of provocative behavior in the Taiwan Strait and planning military expansion after Tokyo and the EU raised concerns about the South China Sea at a UN Security Council meeting.

perspectives.angle: China denounces Japan and EU over South China Sea at UN
perspectives.summary: Beijing accused Tokyo of provocative behavior in the Taiwan Strait and military expansion plans after Japan and the EU raised concerns about South China Sea tensions at a UN Security Council meeting.

headlines.headline: European Shares Subdued as US-Iran Talks Stall
headlines.summary: European stocks were muted as investors weighed a diplomatic impasse in US-Iran negotiations while bracing for central bank meetings and corporate earnings.

perspectives.angle: Dollar eases as US-Iran talks stall ahead of Powell's final Fed meeting
perspectives.summary: The US dollar weakened against the euro as diplomatic impasse in US-Iran negotiations weighed on markets. Investors are also watching for Jerome Powell's expected final Federal Reserve meeting this week.

headlines.headline: Markets See Through Iran War as Permacrisis Becomes Norm
headlines.summary: Record-high stocks amid a geopolitical shock puzzles investors. The dissonance suggests we are now stuck in a world of permanent crisis where markets exploit volatility.

perspectives.angle: Markets see through Iran war as investors adapt to permacrisis
perspectives.summary: Record-high stocks amid the Iran geopolitical shock puzzle analysts. The dissonance suggests investors are either seeing through the conflict or accepting a new normal of constant instability.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Rise as Hormuz Standoff Continues
headlines.summary: Oil prices jumped over 3% as stalled efforts to end the Iran war kept the Strait of Hormuz largely closed, though UAE's OPEC exit trimmed gains.

headlines.headline: First Japanese Crude Tanker Transits Strait of Hormuz Since Middle East Conflict Began
headlines.summary: A Japanese crude oil tanker successfully crossed the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday, marking the first such transit since the outbreak of the Middle East conflict. The development comes as the strategic waterway remains largely blocked amid ongoing tensions.

perspectives.angle: First LNG Tanker Crosses Strait of Hormuz Since Iran War Began
perspectives.summary: A liquefied natural gas tanker managed by UAE state energy company ADNOC has crossed the Strait of Hormuz for the first time since the conflict started, according to ship-tracking data, signaling a potential easing of the blockade.

headlines.headline: Meta-Manus AI Deal Unwinding Would Be Complex and Time-Consuming, Analysts Say
headlines.summary: Beijing blocked Meta's $2 billion acquisition of Chinese AI startup Manus, but unraveling the deal will be difficult given integration progress over four months. Manus staff have moved into Meta offices and technology has been absorbed into Meta's ecosystem.

headlines.headline: UN General Assembly President Calls for Cross-Regional Alliance to Support UN Amid Iran and Gaza Wars
headlines.summary: UNGA President Annalena Baerbock urged a cross-regional alliance to strengthen the United Nations as it navigates simultaneous crises from the Iran and Gaza conflicts.

headlines.headline: US Air Force Funds Final Development of Beehive Industries' Frenzy 8 Engine for Drone Systems
headlines.summary: The USAF awarded $29.7 million to complete development of the additively manufactured 200-lb thrust Frenzy 8 engine, part of a strategy to develop low-cost disposable jet engines for unmanned aerial systems and standoff weapons.

headlines.headline: World Bank Forecasts 24% Surge in Energy Prices
headlines.summary: Energy prices are projected to surge 24% in 2026 to their highest level since Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine, driven by Middle East war disruptions. Commodity prices expected to hit four-year highs.

perspectives.angle: World Bank Forecasts 24% Energy Price Surge in 2026 Due to Iran War
perspectives.summary: Energy prices are projected to jump 24% in 2026, reaching the highest level since Russia's invasion of Ukraine. Brent crude is forecast to average $86/barrel, with potential to reach $115 if hostilities persist and export recovery is slow.

headlines.headline: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Seize Wartime Power
headlines.summary: Two months into war with the US and Israel, Iran's IRGC has displaced the Supreme Leader as the dominant power center, hardening Tehran's stance in ceasefire negotiations.

headlines.headline: Euro Zone Inflation Expectations Surge on Iran War
headlines.summary: Euro zone consumers have sharply raised inflation expectations due to the Iran war fallout, while economic growth is faltering—creating a policy dilemma for the European Central Bank.

headlines.headline: Belarus Frees Polish Journalist in Prisoner Swap
headlines.summary: Belarus released prominent journalist Andrzej Poczobut after five years in prison as part of a 10-person prisoner exchange with Poland, marking a rare diplomatic breakthrough amid ongoing tensions.

headlines.headline: Iranian Oil Tankers Cluster Near Chabahar as U.S. Blockade Tightens
headlines.summary: At least six Iranian tankers laden with oil are loitering near Chabahar port, caught between the Strait of Hormuz and the U.S. naval blockade line. The cluster suggests Iran continues loading oil but the U.S. interception is working to delay exports.

headlines.headline: Ex-Mossad Chief Pardo Condemns West Bank Violence as "Existential Threat" to Israel
headlines.summary: Former Mossad director Tamir Pardo said he was "ashamed to be Jewish" over settler violence in the West Bank, comparing it to Holocaust-era tactics. He warned the crackdown risks civil strife and poses an existential threat to Israel.

headlines.headline: Australia Invests $1.7B in Second HIMARS Regiment for Long-Range Strike
headlines.summary: Australia will spend AUD2.3 billion to establish a second long-range fires regiment equipped with HIMARS launchers and Precision Strike Missiles, capable of hitting targets up to 500 km away, extendable beyond 1,000 km with future PrSM increments.

headlines.headline: Japan Ends Decades-Long Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports to Allies
headlines.summary: Japan has abolished its 1967 ban on lethal weapons exports, opening sales to 17 defense partner countries. The move includes a $6.5B frigate deal with Australia and potential submarine sales to Indonesia, driven by concerns over China and North Korea.

headlines.headline: China Tests Truck-Mounted Nuclear Reactor for AI Data Centers
headlines.summary: Chinese scientists are testing a prototype 10-megawatt vehicle-mounted nuclear reactor that could power medium-sized AI data centers. The "nuclear power bank" offers decades-long operation without recharging and could serve remote regions, ships, and space systems.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Strikes Russian Tuapse Oil Refinery Again
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack caused a major fire at Russia's Tuapse oil refinery on the Black Sea coast, marking the third strike on the facility in under two weeks as Ukraine continues targeting Russian energy infrastructure.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian Tuapse Refinery for Third Time
perspectives.summary: Ukraine targeted the major Russian oil refinery in the Black Sea port of Tuapse for the third time in two weeks, part of sustained drone campaign against Russian energy infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Europe Faces Jet Fuel Supply Crunch as Middle East Imports Stop
headlines.summary: Europe's jet fuel imports from the Middle East have dried up due to the Iran war disrupting supplies, raising concerns of a shortage before the peak summer travel season. Sweden has already warned of potential shortages.

perspectives.angle: Vietnam gas giant shifts LPG imports from Middle East to US amid Iran war
perspectives.summary: PetroVietnam Gas will import 66,000 tonnes of cooking fuel from US in May versus 44,000 from Middle East, as Iran war disrupts Hormuz Strait and forces Asian buyers to find new suppliers.

headlines.headline: Philippines Confident US Commitment to Indo-Pacific Amid Iran War
headlines.summary: The Philippine defense secretary said Manila is not worried about reduced US deterrence in the Indo-Pacific due to the Middle East conflict, though he warned China could try to exploit any perceived opening.

headlines.headline: US Army Launches Sword 26 Exercise to Execute NATO War Plans
headlines.summary: The US Army in Europe kicked off Sword 26, a new training program across eight countries with 15,500 troops aimed at implementing NATO's regional defense plans for the High North and eastern flank, replacing the Defender exercise series.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Official: US Has No Defense Against Hypersonic Weapons
headlines.summary: A senior Pentagon official told Congress the US currently has no defense against hypersonic weapons or advanced cruise missiles, as Trump's $185 billion Golden Dome missile shield program faces scrutiny. China cited as 'pacing competitor'.

headlines.headline: US and Iran Clash at UN as Tehran Gets Nuclear Non-Proliferation Role
headlines.summary: The United States and Iran exchanged sharp accusations at the opening of a month-long UN conference reviewing the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty, with tensions heightened by Iran's election as a vice president of the proceedings.

perspectives.angle: US and Iran Clash at UN Over Nuclear Non-Proliferation Role
perspectives.summary: Tensions flared at the United Nations after Tehran was selected as a vice president for the NPT review conference, prompting a sharp rebuke from Washington over Iran's nuclear program amid the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: German Chancellor Merz Says Iran Is 'Humiliating' the US
headlines.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz criticized the Trump administration's handling of the Iran war, saying Tehran is making US officials travel to Pakistan for talks without results, as European frustration with the conflict grows.

headlines.headline: Zelensky condemns Israel for buying grain stolen by Russia from Ukraine
headlines.summary: President Zelensky publicly accused Israel of purchasing grain stolen by Moscow from occupied Ukrainian territories, announcing Kyiv is preparing sanctions. The move strains Israel-Ukraine relations amid both countries facing separate conflicts.

headlines.headline: Gulf leaders hold emergency summit in Jeddah on Iran conflict
headlines.summary: Gulf leaders are convening an emergency consultative summit in Jeddah to discuss regional tensions, focusing on the Iran conflict, maritime security, and mediation efforts between Washington and Tehran.

headlines.headline: Bahrain sentences five to life for plotting terrorist acts with Iran
headlines.summary: A Bahraini court sentenced five people to life imprisonment for plotting terrorist and hostile acts with Iran, highlighting ongoing regional tensions and Iranian influence operations in Gulf states.

headlines.headline: Gulf stock markets rise despite lingering geopolitical tensions
headlines.summary: Major Gulf stock markets traded higher on Tuesday amid corporate earnings announcements, though investors remained cautious about ongoing regional tensions from the Iran conflict.

perspectives.angle: Gulf Stock Markets Edge Higher Despite Stalled Iran Diplomacy
perspectives.summary: Most Gulf equity markets eked out small gains despite fading hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough in the US-Iran conflict. Hardening positions on both sides underscore the difficulty of reaching a negotiated settlement.

headlines.headline: King Charles to address US Congress in rare speech amid Iran war tensions
headlines.summary: Britain's King Charles will press the importance of unity with the United States in an address to Congress during a state visit, aiming to repair deep divisions between the two countries over the war in Iran.

perspectives.angle: King Charles Addresses US Congress Despite Iran War Divisions
perspectives.summary: Britain's King Charles met with President Trump and delivered a rare address to a joint session of Congress, seeking to underscore US-UK unity despite deep differences over the Iran war.

headlines.headline: Reuters investigation reveals Ukrainian teens accused of betraying country
headlines.summary: More than 1,100 Ukrainians have been accused of betraying their country since Russia's invasion, with one in five being minors. The investigation examines how children can be seen as both perpetrators and victims.

headlines.headline: El Nino weather pattern threatens global energy markets
headlines.summary: Major meteorological services forecast a strong El Nino pattern from next month, expected to impact global temperatures and rainfall trends while spurring shifts in coal and natural gas consumption across key regions.

headlines.headline: Media freedom under sustained attack across EU, report finds
headlines.summary: Journalists face rising threats while media ownership is concentrated in fewer hands, a leading civil liberties group warns. Public trust in media has dropped significantly across European Union member states.

headlines.headline: Republicans brace for brutal midterms as Trump popularity slips
headlines.summary: President Trump's approval ratings are down, gas prices are up, and congressional Republicans are heading to the exits in record numbers while Democratic enthusiasm rises ahead of November midterm elections.

headlines.headline: Israel Establishes Buffer Zone in Southern Lebanon, Displacing Civilians
headlines.summary: Israeli forces have created a "Yellow Line" buffer zone south of the Litani River in southern Lebanon, forcing residents to flee and raising concerns of long-term displacement amid ongoing conflict with Hezbollah.

headlines.headline: Hundreds of Google Employees Protest Pentagon AI Collaboration
headlines.summary: Google staff signed an open letter urging CEO Sundar Pichai not to allow AI tools for classified Pentagon work, warning of potential misuse in lethal autonomous weapons and surveillance.

perspectives.angle: Google employees petition CEO to refuse classified AI work with Pentagon
perspectives.summary: Hundreds of Google AI researchers have signed a letter urging CEO Sundar Pichai to refuse classified military AI work, reigniting debates about tech company involvement in defense projects.

headlines.headline: Russia to Host International Security Forum with 180+ Foreign Delegations
headlines.summary: Moscow will host its first International Security Forum in late May, bringing together security council secretaries, intelligence chiefs, and defense industry representatives from over 180 countries to showcase Russian military technology.

headlines.headline: China Blocks Meta's $2B Acquisition of AI Startup Manus
headlines.summary: China's National Development and Reform Commission blocked Meta's acquisition of AI startup Manus, citing national security concerns. The move signals Beijing's tighter control over advanced technology transfers amid U.S.-China AI rivalry.

perspectives.angle: China Blocks Meta From Acquiring AI Startup Manus
perspectives.summary: Beijing tightened scrutiny of AI industry amid intensifying geopolitical rivalry with the US over technology, moving to block Meta from acquiring the AI startup.

headlines.headline: Australia to Tax Meta, Google, TikTok to Fund Journalism
headlines.summary: Australia proposed legislation to tax digital giants Meta, Google and TikTok unless they negotiate deals to pay local media outlets for news content. The News Bargaining Incentive would charge platforms that refuse to strike voluntary deals.

headlines.headline: UK PM Starmer Faces Parliamentary Probe Over Mandelson Appointment
headlines.summary: Britain's parliament will vote on an inquiry into Prime Minister Keir Starmer over whether he misled the House of Commons about the appointment of Peter Mandelson as U.K. ambassador to the U.S., amid questions about Mandelson's past ties.

headlines.headline: Trump Unhappy With Iran War Proposal, Nuclear Talks Stall
headlines.summary: Iran proposed reopening the Strait of Hormuz while setting aside nuclear discussions until after the war ends. A US official said Trump is not satisfied with the offer, leaving the conflict in diplomatic limbo as oil prices climb.

perspectives.angle: Iran proposes reopening Strait of Hormuz while delaying nuclear talks
perspectives.summary: Tehran offered to restore maritime traffic through the vital waterway and lift the US blockade, but suggested postponing the contentious nuclear issue negotiations to a later stage.

perspectives.angle: Trump Unhappy With Latest Iranian Proposal, US Official Says
perspectives.summary: President Trump expressed dissatisfaction with Iran's latest offer to end the two-month war, as Tehran's proposal did not address nuclear program concerns. Peace talks remain stalled with no breakthrough in sight.

headlines.headline: Moodys Upgrades China Outlook to Stable on Economic Resilience
headlines.summary: Moodys revised Chinas outlook from negative to stable, citing resilient economic and fiscal strength despite domestic pressures and ongoing trade and geopolitical challenges.

perspectives.angle: Moody's Upgrades China Outlook to Stable, Cites Economic Resilience
perspectives.summary: Credit ratings agency Moody's revised China's outlook from negative to stable, pointing to resilient economic and fiscal strength despite ongoing trade pressures and geopolitical challenges.

headlines.headline: Iran War Fertilizer Squeeze Threatens Global Grain Harvests
headlines.summary: Farmers worldwide face a second fertilizer price surge in four years due to the Iran war. With grain prices too low to cushion the blow, many are reconsidering planting plans, risking global food production.

headlines.headline: Bank of Japan Holds Rates as Three Board Members Push for Hike
headlines.summary: The BOJ kept interest rates steady but three of nine board members proposed raising borrowing costs, reflecting concerns over inflationary pressures from the Middle East conflict.

perspectives.angle: Bank of Japan holds rates steady in split vote as Iran war fuels inflation concerns
perspectives.summary: The BOJ kept its benchmark rate at 0.75% but three of nine board members proposed a hike, signaling growing concern over inflationary pressures from Middle East conflict disrupting energy markets.

headlines.headline: North Korea Executions Doubled During COVID Pandemic
headlines.summary: A new report shows executions and death sentences more than doubled in North Korea after border closures in 2020, with public killings surging for offenses like consuming South Korean media. The regime appears to be intensifying state violence to suppress growing internal discontent.

headlines.headline: IDF Destroys Over 1,000 Hezbollah Sites in Southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: Israeli forces dismantled more than 1,000 Hezbollah infrastructure sites and uncovered weapons caches including anti-tank missiles and mortar shells. The operations come amid escalating tensions with Hezbollah rejecting ceasefire terms.

headlines.headline: South Korea Calls North Korea Most Pressing Nuclear Challenge
headlines.summary: At the NPT review conference in New York, South Korea's nuclear envoy emphasized North Korea's unique threat to nonproliferation and urged Russia to end military cooperation with Pyongyang. Seoul signaled a shift away from CVID toward a phased denuclearization approach.

headlines.headline: Iran revises school strike death toll to 155, including 120 children
headlines.summary: Iran reported 73 boys and 47 girls killed in a February 28 strike on an elementary school in Minab, down from earlier reports of over 175 dead. A US Tomahawk missile hit the school due to a targeting mistake, according to a preliminary military investigation.

headlines.headline: Iran's economy devastated by US-Israel strikes, millions of jobs at risk
headlines.summary: US and Israeli airstrikes damaged 20,000 factories (20% of Iran production). Over 1 million jobs lost directly, with 10-12 million more at risk. Steel and petrochemical sectors crippled. Food prices have surged 68-75% as blockade chokes imports.

headlines.headline: Lithuania Charges 13 People in GRU-Linked Assassination Attempts
headlines.summary: Lithuanian authorities charged 13 individuals from multiple countries with two attempted murders in Vilnius tied to Russia's GRU military intelligence agency, marking a significant escalation in Russian intelligence operations targeting opponents abroad.

perspectives.angle: Lithuania charges 13 people with attempted murders linked to Russian GRU
perspectives.summary: Lithuania has charged 13 individuals from multiple countries with two attempted murders in Vilnius connected to Russia's GRU military intelligence agency, marking a significant escalation in allegations of Russian intelligence operations on NATO territory.

headlines.headline: India and New Zealand Sign Major Free Trade Agreement
headlines.summary: India and New Zealand signed a comprehensive free trade agreement that will cut or eliminate tariffs on 95% of goods traded between them, as both nations seek to diversify trade relationships amid global disruptions from the Iran war and US tariff policies.

headlines.headline: Global Military Spending Surges to Record $2.9 Trillion
headlines.summary: World military expenditure reached an all-time high of $2.89 trillion in 2025, driven by Europe's rearmament push and rising tensions in Asia, marking the eleventh consecutive year of growth according to SIPRI data.

perspectives.angle: Global Military Spending Surges Despite US Ukraine Aid Freeze
perspectives.summary: Global military expenditure rose 2.9% in 2025, driven by defense spending increases in Europe and Asia, even as US military aid to Ukraine was frozen. The trend reflects growing security concerns worldwide.

headlines.headline: Japan PM Launches Defense Policy Review Panel Amid Rising Threats
headlines.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi inaugurated a panel to review national security and defense policies as tensions escalate with China, North Korea, and Russia in the Indo-Pacific region.

perspectives.angle: Japan Seeks Deeper Defense Ties with Australia Amid Indo-Pacific Tensions
perspectives.summary: Japan's Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi pledged to deepen defense cooperation with Australia at a Nikkei forum in Perth, citing an "increasingly severe" security environment in the Indo-Pacific as regional tensions mount.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Rivals Announce Alliance to Challenge Israeli Government
headlines.summary: Two of Benjamin Netanyahu's top rivals announced they would join forces in upcoming elections to challenge his coalition, focusing on domestic issues including military conscription for ultra-Orthodox Israelis.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu's Rivals Bennett and Lapid Join Forces for Israeli Election
perspectives.summary: Two of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's main political opponents, former PMs Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid, announced they are merging parties to challenge his coalition in elections expected later this year.

headlines.headline: Oil prices climb 1% as Iran war stalemate persists
headlines.summary: Brent crude rose toward $110 per barrel as diplomatic efforts to end the US-Iran war appeared stalled, with the Strait of Hormuz remaining effectively closed to shipping.

perspectives.angle: China's Clean Tech Exporters See Record Sales as Iran War Disrupts Oil Flows
perspectives.summary: Chinese exporters of batteries, solar systems, and electric vehicles posted record sales as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz cut off Middle Eastern oil and gas supplies, driving demand for alternative energy.

perspectives.angle: Oil Prices Climb Nearly 2% as US-Iran Peace Talks Stall
perspectives.summary: Crude oil extended gains as diplomatic efforts between Washington and Tehran faltered while shipments through the Strait of Hormuz remained limited, tightening global supplies.

headlines.headline: Ukraine summons Israeli ambassador over Russian grain shipments
headlines.summary: Kyiv warned Israel about allowing grain shipments from Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories, with Foreign Minister Sybiha saying Israel must prevent "stolen" Ukrainian grain from entering its ports.

headlines.headline: Zelenskiy rejects EU associate membership offer, demands full accession by 2027
headlines.summary: Ukraine's president rejected a German-French proposal for associate membership at a Cyprus summit, insisting on full EU membership by 2027. German Chancellor Merz suggested Ukraine may need to accept territorial losses to advance EU prospects.

headlines.headline: US Pressures Taiwan Parliament to Pass Comprehensive Defense Budget
headlines.summary: The top U.S. diplomat in Taiwan urged the opposition-majority parliament to approve a comprehensive defense budget, emphasizing the critical need for integrated air and missile defense systems amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Iran War Hands OPEC's Swing Producer Crown to America
headlines.summary: The United States has emerged as the world's dominant energy superpower, stepping in to shield the global economy from the oil crunch triggered by the Iran conflict through increased exports and strategic reserve releases.

headlines.headline: Italy Extradites Chinese Cyber-Espionage Suspect to US
headlines.summary: Italy has extradited Xu Zewei, a 34-year-old alleged Chinese state-sponsored hacker, to the US where he faces charges for stealing COVID-19 vaccine research during the pandemic as part of the "Hafnium" cyber-espionage campaign targeting universities and immunologists.

headlines.headline: 50+ Nations Launch First-Ever Fossil Fuel Exit Talks in Colombia
headlines.summary: More than 50 governments including major producers like Canada, Norway, Australia and Brazil are meeting in Santa Marta for the inaugural conference on phasing out fossil fuels, bypassing UN climate talks to address the main driver of global warming amid the Iran war energy crunch.

headlines.headline: Lebanese Minister Says Hezbollah Disarmament Must Happen
headlines.summary: Lebanon's Social Affairs Minister Haneen Sayed told a CSIS forum in Washington that Hezbollah disarmament "must happen" and is a government demand, but cautioned it would require a political settlement and time given the group's role as a "community" not just a militant organization.

headlines.headline: CATL Shares Drop 7% After Announcing $5 Billion Renewable Expansion Fund
headlines.summary: World's largest EV battery maker CATL announced a HK$39.2 billion (US$5 billion) private placement to expand its renewables business, with shares falling nearly 7% as investors reacted to the dilution amid global oil price surges from the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Indonesia Train Collision Kills 14 Near Jakarta
headlines.summary: A long-distance train crashed into a commuter train outside Jakarta, killing at least 14 people and injuring 84. Rescue teams worked to free passengers trapped in the women-only carriage. The accident raises fresh concerns about Indonesia's aging rail infrastructure.

perspectives.angle: Indonesia Train Crash Kills 14, Including Women-Only Carriage Victims
perspectives.summary: A commuter train collision near Jakarta killed 14 people, mostly women in a women-only carriage, with 84 injured. President Prabowo ordered an investigation and admitted large parts of the rail network are poorly maintained.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Accused of Bombing Kunar University in Afghanistan
headlines.summary: Pakistani air strikes on Kunar province killed at least 7 people and wounded 75, including university students. Taliban condemned the attack as 'war crimes,' with reports of jets, drones, and mortars targeting civilian areas.

perspectives.angle: Pakistan Accused of Airstrikes on Afghan University
perspectives.summary: Taliban officials say Pakistani attacks on Kunar province killed at least 7 people and injured 75, including students at Kunar University. Pakistan denied the reports. The attacks threaten a fragile ceasefire brokered by China in April.

headlines.headline: Suspect in Washington Dinner Shooting Charged With Attempted Assassination of Trump
headlines.summary: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, was charged with attempting to assassinate President Trump after opening fire at the White House Correspondents' Dinner. He faces life in prison if convicted.

perspectives.angle: Suspect charged with attempted assassination of Trump after correspondents dinner shooting
perspectives.summary: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, was charged with attempted assassination after allegedly trying to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner with weapons. He made his first federal court appearance.

headlines.headline: NATO Considers Ending Annual Summits Amid Trump Tensions
headlines.summary: Six sources told Reuters that NATO is weighing whether to end its practice of annual summits, potentially avoiding a tense encounter with Trump in his final year as questions mount over the alliance's future.

perspectives.angle: NATO considers ending annual summits amid tensions with Trump administration
perspectives.summary: Six sources tell Reuters the alliance may skip 2028 summit to avoid tense encounter in Trump final year, with some members pushing to hold summits every two years instead of annually.

headlines.headline: Russian Drone Strike on Odesa Wounds 14 Including Children
headlines.summary: An overnight Russian drone attack on Ukraine's southern port city wounded over a dozen people, including two children, and damaged residential buildings as the war continues.

perspectives.angle: Russian Drone Strike Wounds 10 Including Children in Odesa
perspectives.summary: An overnight Russian drone attack on Ukraine's southern port city of Odesa wounded 10 people including two children and damaged residential buildings. The strike comes amid continued fighting.

headlines.headline: Afghanistan risks losing 25,000 female teachers and health workers by 2030
headlines.summary: UNICEF warns that Taliban restrictions on girls education and women employment could eliminate 25% of Afghanistan 2021 female workforce, affecting 1 million girls currently with projections to double by 2030.

headlines.headline: Regional police crackdown arrests 326 in Asia-wide child abuse probe
headlines.summary: Singapore, Brunei, Hong Kong, Japan, Malaysia, South Korea and Thailand conducted a four-week operation arresting 326 people and investigating 119 more across 382 locations for online child sexual exploitation.

headlines.headline: Zelensky orders 50,000 ground robots for Ukraine military
headlines.summary: Ukraine president announces major expansion of unmanned ground systems for logistics, evacuations and combat, following widespread drone adoption as the next step in modern warfare.

headlines.headline: Trump Administration Fires Entire National Science Board
headlines.summary: All 22 members of the National Science Board, the independent body overseeing the National Science Foundation, received termination notices from the Presidential Personnel Office. Scientists warn the move could enable deep cuts to fundamental research funding.

headlines.headline: Taiwan on alert as Chinese warships approach Penghu Islands
headlines.summary: Taiwan detected a Chinese destroyer and frigate operating near the Penghu islands, home to major Taiwanese military bases, prompting Taipei to deploy naval and air forces to monitor the vessels.

headlines.headline: German Chancellor links Ukraine peace to territorial concessions
headlines.summary: Friedrich Merz suggested Ukraine may need to cede territory to Russia as part of a peace deal, framing such concessions as a potential pathway to EU membership for Kyiv.

headlines.headline: North Korea opens memorial for troops killed in Ukraine war
headlines.summary: Kim Jong Un inaugurated a memorial museum honoring North Korean soldiers killed fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, pledging continued support for Moscow's war effort during a ceremony attended by Russia's defense minister.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo meets first female Archbishop of Canterbury in historic encounter
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV prayed with Archbishop of Canterbury Sarah Mullally at the Vatican, vowing to overcome differences in a historic meeting with the first female leader of the Church of England and the global Anglican Communion.

headlines.headline: Bahrain strips 69 people of citizenship over Iran support
headlines.summary: Bahrain revoked citizenship of 69 people for what it called sympathy with Iran's hostile acts and collaboration with foreign entities. Rights groups condemned the move as a 'blatant abuse of power'.

perspectives.angle: Bahrain strips 69 people of citizenship over Iran sympathy during war
perspectives.summary: State news agency reports citizenship revocations include families of those accused of praising Iran hostile acts during the US-Israel conflict with Iran.

headlines.headline: Putin Pledges Support for Iran in St. Petersburg Talks with Foreign Minister Araghchi
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in St. Petersburg, offering support to navigate the "difficult period" while expressing hope for peace. Moscow has resisted being drawn into the Middle East conflict while continuing its invasion of Ukraine.

perspectives.angle: Putin Hosts Iranian FM Araghchi, Pledges Support as US-Iran Talks Stall
perspectives.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin hosted Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in Moscow, promising support as Iran faces pressure over the Strait of Hormuz blockade. The meeting comes amid failed US-Iran negotiations over ending the conflict.

headlines.headline: Lebanese President Aoun Rejects "Wars Serving Foreign Interests" in Rebuke to Hezbollah
headlines.summary: President Joseph Aoun pushed back against critics of negotiations with Israel, declaring that "traitors are those who drag Lebanon into a war to serve foreign interests" — a direct criticism of Hezbollah's wars "in support of Gaza and Iran" rather than Lebanon's interests.

headlines.headline: Australia Coalition Proposes $800M Plan to Double Fuel Reserves
headlines.summary: Australia's federal Coalition unveiled an $800 million plan to double the nation's minimum fuel reserves to 60 days, citing energy security concerns amid the Middle East conflict. The proposal adds at least one billion litres of new onshore storage with a focus on diesel.

headlines.headline: US reviews latest Iranian proposal to end war stalemate
headlines.summary: President Trump discussed a new Iranian proposal with his national security team as Tehran's foreign minister headed to Russia. The proposal reportedly offers to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for ending the US blockade.

headlines.headline: US Supreme Court reinstates Texas electoral map favoring Republicans
headlines.summary: The conservative-majority Supreme Court reinstated a redrawn Texas electoral map that could flip up to five Democratic-held House seats to Republicans. The ruling overturned a lower court decision that had found the map racially discriminatory.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo meets first female Archbishop of Canterbury in historic Vatican encounter
headlines.summary: Pope Leo and Archbishop Sarah Mullally met for the first time at the Vatican, exchanging gifts and praying together in a symbolic step toward unity between the Catholic Church and Church of England.

headlines.headline: IDF Chief Condemns Unethical Soldier Conduct as Discipline Erodes
headlines.summary: Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir warned that discipline has eroded in recent wars, citing incidents including soldiers smashing a Jesus statue in Lebanon and wearing unapproved patches.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Completes Major US Tank Deal With Final Abrams Shipment
headlines.summary: The acquisition marks the biggest upgrade to Taiwan armored forces in decades as it bolsters northern defense capabilities against Chinese threats.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan Receives Final Batch of M1A2T Abrams Tanks From US
perspectives.summary: Taiwan has received the last of its 108 M1A2T Abrams tanks from the United States, completing a .28 billion procurement deal that strengthens the island’s armored capabilities.

headlines.headline: Iran ceasefire negotiations stall as both sides dig in on demands
headlines.summary: Diplomatic efforts to end the Iran war have stalled over the weekend, with both Iran and the US maintaining hardline positions. The stalemate comes two months into the conflict that has disrupted global oil supplies and heightened regional tensions.

perspectives.angle: US-Iran Conflict Stuck in No War No Peace Limbo as Both Sides Wait
perspectives.summary: With peace talks stalled and neither side willing to escalate further, the US-Iran standoff has settled into an uneasy stalemate where each is betting it can outlast the other.

headlines.headline: Iran war may accelerate international talks to exit fossil fuels
headlines.summary: The Iran conflict has disrupted oil and gas supplies and sent prices spiking, potentially invigorating discussions at IMO and Santa Marta meetings on accelerating the energy transition. Countries worldwide are reconsidering their approach to energy security.

headlines.headline: Asia Could Emerge as Unexpected Winner from Iran War Fallout
headlines.summary: Despite being heavily reliant on Middle East energy, Asia may benefit long-term from accelerated cybersecurity investment, fossil fuel diversification, and supply chain restructuring prompted by the conflict.

perspectives.angle: Asia May Emerge as Unexpected Beneficiary from Iran War Fallout
perspectives.summary: While Asia has been highly vulnerable to the Iran war due to its dependence on Middle East energy, analysts suggest the region could emerge as a long-term winner from trends accelerated by the conflict, including supply chain diversification and energy transition.

headlines.headline: North Korea and Russia Deepen Alliance at Memorial for Fallen Soldiers in Ukraine
headlines.summary: At a ceremony honoring North Korean soldiers killed fighting in Ukraine, Moscow offered to renew a military pact with Pyongyang, strengthening the anti-West coalition as the war drags on.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Disrupts Russian-Linked Network Planning Assassinations and Sabotage Across EU
headlines.summary: Prosecutors announced an international operation disrupted a network preparing killings, arson, and infrastructure attacks across multiple European countries, linked to Russian intelligence.

headlines.headline: US Army Developing Heavier Hybrid-Powered Infantry Squad Vehicle
headlines.summary: The Army is testing a new hybrid-powered ISV-H vehicle designed to carry more armor and equipment while maintaining mobility for infantry squads in rugged terrain.

headlines.headline: Taiwan president cancels Africa visit after China pressures three nations to deny flight permits
headlines.summary: President Lai Ching-te postponed his visit to Eswatini after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar withdrew flight permissions under Chinese pressure, in what Taiwan calls blatant interference in other countries affairs.

headlines.headline: US military seizes another Iranian oil tanker in Indian Ocean standoff
headlines.summary: The Pentagon seized the Guyana-flagged tanker Majestic X in the Indian Ocean, the latest move in the escalating maritime conflict with Iran that has effectively choked off oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Chinas Xi meets Taiwan opposition leader ahead of Trump summit
headlines.summary: Xi Jinping held a rare meeting with Kuomintang leader Cheng Li-wun in Beijing, calling for peaceful cross-strait development ahead of a May summit with Trump where Taiwan arms sales are expected to be a key topic.

headlines.headline: Worker killed at Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant in Ukrainian drone attack
headlines.summary: An employee of the Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant was killed in a Ukrainian drone attack, according to the Russian-installed management of the station.

perspectives.angle: Russia attacks Odesa with drones, claims Ukraine hit Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant
perspectives.summary: Russian drone attacks on Odesa injured 11 people including children, while a Ukrainian drone strike killed a worker at the Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant, escalating tensions around Europes largest nuclear facility.

headlines.headline: Germany plans to build Europe's strongest army, says defence editor
headlines.summary: The Economist examines Germany's ambitious plan to build Europe's strongest military, analyzing whether the country can achieve its goals and what obstacles stand in the way.

headlines.headline: North Korea's Kim pledges continued support for Russia's policies
headlines.summary: Kim Jong Un said North Korea would continue supporting Russia's policies and discussed bolstering military ties with Moscow, signaling cooperation will endure beyond the Ukraine war.

perspectives.angle: North Korea Opens Memorial Museum for Soldiers Killed in Russia-Ukraine War
perspectives.summary: Pyongyang unveiled a memorial honoring North Korean troops killed fighting for Russia in Kursk. Kim Jong Un pledged continued support for Moscow's "sacred war" against Ukraine.

perspectives.angle: Kim Jong Un Vows Continued Support for Russia at Memorial Ceremony
perspectives.summary: At the opening of a memorial for North Korean soldiers killed in Kursk, Kim Jong Un reaffirmed support for Russia's invasion of Ukraine and vowed to help Moscow achieve victory.

headlines.headline: Congo creates US-UAE backed paramilitary guard to secure critical minerals
headlines.summary: Congo announced a $100 million paramilitary mining guard backed by US and UAE funding to secure its vast mining operations, as Washington seeks access to critical minerals like coltan amid eastern conflict.

headlines.headline: Poland's Sikorski proposes EU-funded rapid reaction force for regional crises
headlines.summary: Poland's foreign minister calls for a European rapid reaction force funded by the EU to handle neighborhood security operations, arguing Europe must become a more credible ally amid growing geopolitical risks.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Mortar Attacks Kill Four in Afghanistan, Taliban Says
headlines.summary: Mortar and rocket attacks launched by Pakistan against Afghanistan killed at least four people and injured 70 more, according to the Taliban government. The strikes mark another escalation in cross-border tensions between the neighbors.

perspectives.angle: Pakistan-Afghanistan border clashes kill 4, wound 70
perspectives.summary: Mortar and rocket attacks by Pakistan against Afghanistan killed four people and injured 70 on Monday, the Taliban government said. Cross-border violence has escalated amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Water Dispute Kills 42 in Eastern Chad as Conflict Escalates
headlines.summary: At least 42 people were killed in eastern Chad after a dispute between two families over a water well escalated into a cycle of reprisal attacks. The government deployed the military to contain the violence as resource conflicts intensify.

headlines.headline: South Sudan Plane Crash Kills All 14 On Board Near Juba
headlines.summary: A Cessna aircraft crashed southwest of South Sudan's capital Juba, killing all 14 people on board. The country's civil aviation authority said the crash occurred during adverse weather conditions.

headlines.headline: Colombia Offers Record $1.4M Reward for Rebel Leader Behind Deadly Bombing
headlines.summary: Colombia's government announced a record reward for information leading to the capture of a rebel leader known as "Marlon," accused of orchestrating a bus bombing that killed 20 people. The attack marks escalating pre-election violence.

headlines.headline: Israeli Strikes Hit East Lebanon, Expanding Conflict Zone Despite Ceasefire
headlines.summary: The Israeli military begins strikes in eastern Lebanon, expanding the scope of its bombing campaign during a ceasefire that has failed to halt hostilities with Hezbollah.

perspectives.angle: Israeli strikes expand to eastern Lebanon as ceasefire continues to fray
perspectives.summary: The Israeli military expanded its bombing campaign to eastern Lebanon, striking over 20 Hezbollah targets. At least 14 people were killed including two children, as both sides accused each other of violating the US-mediated ceasefire.

headlines.headline: OpenAI Ends Microsoft Exclusivity, Opens Path to Amazon and Google Cloud Deals
headlines.summary: Microsoft loses exclusive access to OpenAI technology, allowing the ChatGPT creator to sell products across rival cloud platforms in a major shift for the AI industry.

headlines.headline: Iran Oil Tankers Forced Back by US Blockade as Hormuz Traffic Dwindles
headlines.summary: Six tankers loaded with Iranian oil were forced to return to Iran by the US naval blockade in recent days, with ship-tracking data showing only minimal traffic through the strategic Strait of Hormuz waterway.

perspectives.angle: Nuclear program deadlock and Hormuz blockade cripple Iran peace efforts
perspectives.summary: Peace talks remain stalled as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed. The Israeli attack that started the war and subsequent nuclear program disputes have created a deadlock with no clear path forward.

headlines.headline: German Chancellor Merz Says Iran Humiliating US in Stalled Peace Talks
headlines.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz criticized the lack of progress in US-Iran negotiations, saying Tehran is making American officials travel to Pakistan and leave without results while the Iranian leadership continues to dictate terms.

perspectives.angle: German Chancellor Merz Blasts Iran for "Humiliating" US with Failed Negotiations
perspectives.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz criticized Iran for letting US officials travel to Islamabad only to leave without progress, saying "an entire nation is being humiliated by the Iranian leadership." His unusually blunt rebuke highlights growing Western frustration with Tehran's negotiating tactics.

headlines.headline: Ukraine and Norway to jointly produce mid-strike drones
headlines.summary: Ukraine and Norway will launch joint production of mid-range strike drones following a defense declaration signed in Oslo. The project expands Ukraine's drone manufacturing capabilities amid the ongoing war.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine unveils new AI-powered strike drones Anubis and Seth-X for deep missions
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian firm Airlogix revealed two new drones: Anubis for deep-strike missions with stealth characteristics, and Seth-X designed for swarm attacks, expanding Ukraine's AI strike capabilities in contested environments.

headlines.headline: Iran Suspends Steel Exports Through May Amid War-Related Strikes
headlines.summary: Iran has banned exports of steel slabs and sheets until May 30, as the country's steel industry faces ongoing strikes during the conflict with Israel and the United States.

headlines.headline: Mediators Continue Push to Bridge US-Iran Gaps After Failed Face-to-Face Talks
headlines.summary: Pakistan-based mediators are still working to bridge gaps between the US and Iran despite the failure of direct diplomacy after Trump called off his envoys' trip over the weekend.

perspectives.angle: Iran FM blames US for failed talks after arriving in Russia
perspectives.summary: Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi blamed Washington excessive demands for the collapse of Islamabad negotiations, arriving in Saint Petersburg to meet Putin. Iran passed written red lines to Americans via Pakistan including nuclear and Hormuz issues.

headlines.headline: Record Government Debt to Persist as Military Spending Rises Globally
headlines.summary: With government debt at historic highs across developed nations and rising military spending needs, fiscal watchdogs are being warned that calls for fiscal discipline will likely go unheeded.

headlines.headline: Israel Withholds Palestinian Clearance Funds, Deducts $200 Million
headlines.summary: Israel has decided to withhold clearance funds to the Palestinian Authority and deducted approximately $200 million this month, amid ongoing tensions over governance and security in the occupied territories.

headlines.headline: Germany's Merz Says US Has 'No Exit Strategy' in Iran War
headlines.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz criticized the US for lacking an exit strategy in the Iran conflict, calling Tehran a "skilled negotiator" as diplomatic efforts remain stalled.

headlines.headline: Ukraine to Help Poland Build Modern Drone Fleet Against Aerial Threats
headlines.summary: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk announced Ukraine will assist Poland in building a "modern drone armada," citing Kyivs proven expertise in defending against aerial attacks.

headlines.headline: Palestinians Use Gaza Rubble to Restore Streets as US Rebuilding Plan Stalls
headlines.summary: Palestinians are crushing concrete and reusing war rubble to repave streets destroyed during Israels two-year assault on Gaza, as US-backed reconstruction efforts remain stalled.

headlines.headline: Germany Suspects Russia Behind Signal Phishing Attacks on Politicians
headlines.summary: German government suspects Russia is behind phishing attacks on Signal accounts targeting high-ranking politicians, including two ministers, military personnel and journalists. Around 300 accounts in the political sphere were compromised through fake security chatbot messages.

headlines.headline: China Expands Economic Toolkit During Trade Truce With US
headlines.summary: China has used its trade truce with the United States to broaden its legal leverage, supply chain and critical technology controls, and expand its toolkit of retaliatory economic measures ahead of a summit between the nations next month.

perspectives.angle: China Uses Trade Truce With US to Expand Economic Leverage Before Summit
perspectives.summary: Beijing has used the trade truce with Washington to broaden its legal leverage, supply chain controls, and critical technology restrictions, expanding its toolkit of retaliatory economic measures ahead of a potential summit between the nations next month.

headlines.headline: Merz Suggests Ukraine May Accept Territorial Loss for EU Path
headlines.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said Ukraine may need to accept that some territory remains outside Kyiv's control in a future peace deal with Russia, linking such concessions to EU membership prospects.

headlines.headline: Iran Submits Proposal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz Without Nuclear Deal
headlines.summary: Iran handed the US a proposal through Pakistani mediators to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war, postponing nuclear negotiations for later. The move comes as US-Iran talks remain stalled.

perspectives.angle: Iran Offers Deal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz While Deferring Nuclear Talks
perspectives.summary: Iran has proposed a new deal to the US through Pakistani mediators that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for postponing nuclear negotiations to a later phase. The diplomatic outreach comes as Iranian FM Araghchi meets with Putin in St. Petersburg amid stalled US-Iran peace talks.

perspectives.angle: Iran Submits Proposal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
perspectives.summary: Iran has submitted a new proposal through Pakistani mediators to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war, postponing nuclear negotiations for a later stage, Axios reported citing US officials.

headlines.headline: Starmer and Trump Discuss Urgent Need to Restore Hormuz Shipping
headlines.summary: British PM Keir Starmer and US President Donald Trump discussed the urgent need to restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz in a call, as the closure continues to disrupt global oil supplies.

headlines.headline: Israel Sent Iron Dome System and Troops to UAE During Iran War
headlines.summary: Israel deployed an Iron Dome air defence system along with troops to operate it in the UAE during the recent conflict with Iran, marking unprecedented security cooperation between Israel and Gulf states.

perspectives.angle: Israel sent Iron Dome and troops to UAE during Iran war
perspectives.summary: Axios reports Israel deployed an Iron Dome battery and personnel to the UAE amid the Iran conflict, deepening defense ties between the countries.

headlines.headline: German Defense Spending Drives European Military Budgets to Decades-High Levels
headlines.summary: Military spending in Europe increased at its fastest pace in nearly 75 years in 2025, largely driven by German rearmament efforts amid the ongoing security crisis.

headlines.headline: US sanctions hit Chinese refiners linked to Iranian oil trade
headlines.summary: US sanctions against Hengli Petrochemical over alleged Iranian crude purchases triggered a sharp 10% stock drop. China largest independent refiner denied the allegations, saying it has never engaged in any trade with Iran.

headlines.headline: China delivers record LNG carrier challenging South Korean shipbuilding dominance
headlines.summary: China Merchants Heavy Industry delivered the 180,000-cubic-meter Celsius Georgetown, marking the fifth Chinese yard capable of building advanced LNG carriers as Beijing intensifies competition with Seoul in high-value shipbuilding.

headlines.headline: Mali defense minister killed in insurgent assault
headlines.summary: Mali's Defense Minister Sadio Camara was killed in coordinated attacks by insurgents including al Qaeda's West Africa affiliate, marking a major blow to the government.

perspectives.angle: Mali Defence Minister Killed in Car Bomb Attack
perspectives.summary: Mali Defence Minister Sadio Camara and family members were killed in a car bomb attack as Tuareg separatists and al-Qaeda affiliate launch a string of coordinated attacks across the country.

headlines.headline: DeepSeek releases new AI model to muted market response
headlines.summary: Chinese startup DeepSeek previewed its next-generation AI model, but market reaction was subdued compared to last year's breakthrough. The fast-moving AI industry continues to evolve rapidly.

headlines.headline: At least 42 killed in Chad water resource clashes
headlines.summary: A clash between two families over a water point in eastern Chad escalated into reprisal attacks, killing at least 42 people amid growing resource scarcity in the Sahel region.

headlines.headline: Iranian Foreign Minister Heads to Moscow as Trump Says Call Us
headlines.summary: Iran's top diplomat Abbas Araghchi arrived in Russia seeking support from Putin as President Trump said Iran could simply call if it wants to negotiate an end to the two-month war.

perspectives.angle: Trump Urges Iran to Call US Directly as Putin Hosts Iranian FM in St. Petersburg
perspectives.summary: President Donald Trump publicly urged Iran to call the United States directly to restart negotiations, while Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met with Russian President Vladimir Putin in St. Petersburg amid stalled diplomatic efforts.

headlines.headline: China Slams EU Industrial Policy as Discriminatory, Mulls Countermeasures
headlines.summary: Beijing has sharply criticized the EU proposed industrial law, calling it a serious barrier and institutional discrimination against Chinese firms. China is considering retaliatory measures in response.

headlines.headline: North Korea and Russia Deepen Military Alliance at Troop Memorial Ceremony
headlines.summary: At a memorial for North Korean soldiers killed fighting in Ukraine, Moscow offered to renew its military pact with Pyongyang. Kim Jong Un and Russian Defense Minister Belousov unveiled the monument together.

headlines.headline: Indian Startup Develops Satellite-Based Missile Launch Detection System
headlines.summary: An Indian firm is developing space-based thermal observation systems designed to detect missile launches in hotspots, representing a significant advance in indigenous defense technology.

headlines.headline: Seven Dead in Major Russian Attack on Ukraine
headlines.summary: At least seven people were killed in Russian strikes across Ukraine, including five in Dnipro where an apartment building was hit. The attack involved over 600 drones and missiles, one of the largest in days.

perspectives.angle: Seven Dead as Russian Strike Hits Residential Building in Dnipro
perspectives.summary: A major Russian attack on Ukraine killed at least seven people, with the city of Dnipro hardest hit after a strike on a residential building. Four deaths were reported from the Dnipro attack alone.

headlines.headline: Five Pro-Palestine Activists Face Trial for Elbit Factory Raid in Germany
headlines.summary: The trial of five European activists accused of attacking a factory linked to Israeli weapons manufacturer Elbit Systems begins in Germany. Prosecutors seek lengthy sentences, calling it a criminal organization act.

headlines.headline: Israelis Question Netanyahu Leadership as Iran War Goals Unfulfilled
headlines.summary: With Iran's government still standing and Hezbollah and Hamas not defeated, many Israelis express dissatisfaction with Netanyahu's wartime leadership ahead of elections scheduled for later this year.

headlines.headline: Can King Charles help heal the US-British rupture?
headlines.summary: Not since Queen Elizabeth II traveled to Washington after the Suez Crisis has a British monarch's visit come at such a fraught time in Anglo-American relations, with the King seeking to restore strained ties.

headlines.headline: 20,000 Seafarers Stranded in Persian Gulf as Hormuz Remains Blocked
headlines.summary: Approximately 20,000 seafarers on hundreds of vessels remain stranded in the Persian Gulf, unable to cross the effectively closed Strait of Hormuz. The UN reports at least 10 seafarers have been killed since the conflict began.

headlines.headline: Four Candidates Vie to Succeed UN Secretary-General Guterres
headlines.summary: Michelle Bachelet (Chile), Rafael Grossi (Argentina), Rebeca Grynspan (Costa Rica), and Macky Sall (Senegal) presented their visions to lead the UN, focusing on restoring global peace and reforming the institution.

headlines.headline: Iran War Taking Toll on Chinese Manufacturing Economy
headlines.summary: The Middle East conflict is putting pressure on Chinese factory orders, costs and jobs as the Strait of Hormuz disruption hits exports. Costs have risen around 20% for some traders, adding strain to an economy already dealing with US tariffs and slower growth.

headlines.headline: Erdogan Offers Support to Trump After White House Correspondents Dinner Shooting
headlines.summary: Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan called President Donald Trump to offer support following a shooting incident at the White House Correspondents Association dinner. A gunman opened fire near the event attended by Trump and senior officials.

headlines.headline: Chinese EVs surge in South Korea with 33% market share as fuel prices climb
headlines.summary: China-made electric vehicles now account for one in three new registrations in South Korea, up from 4.7% in 2022, driven by Tesla Shanghai models and competitive pricing amid elevated fuel costs linked to the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Trump Cancels US Peace Delegation Trip to Pakistan After Iranian FM Departs
headlines.summary: President Trump canceled Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushners planned trip to Islamabad for Iran peace talks, suggesting negotiations continue by phone instead.

headlines.headline: Security Under Review After Gunman Breaches White House Correspondents Dinner
headlines.summary: Law enforcement officials are reassessing security protocols after an armed suspect was able to get close to an event attended by President Trump and senior officials, raising questions about protection arrangements.

perspectives.angle: White House Correspondents' Dinner Shooting Suspect Appears in Federal Court
perspectives.summary: The suspect who opened fire at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, where President Trump and top officials were present, appeared in federal court. Authorities believe Trump and officials were likely targets of the attack.

perspectives.angle: Suspect in Press Dinner Shooting Wrote Anti-Christian Manifesto
perspectives.summary: The suspect in the White House Correspondents Dinner shooting, identified as Cole Allen, wrote an anti-Christian manifesto according to President Trump. The 31-year-old from California was armed with a shotgun.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo and the Shift From Just War to Peace Activism
headlines.summary: The popes criticism of the Iran war reflects a broader transformation in Catholic teaching on warfare, driven by the devastating power of modern weaponry and the collapse of traditional just war theory.

headlines.headline: Iranian Foreign Minister Returns to Pakistan, Seeks to Revive Ceasefire Talks
headlines.summary: Iran's Abbas Araghchi made a brief return to Islamabad as Pakistani leaders scrambled to reignite negotiations. The diplomatic push comes amid continued hostilities and a stalled Strait of Hormuz blockade.

perspectives.angle: Trump Meets Team to Discuss Iranian Proposal as Foreign Minister Visits Putin
perspectives.summary: The US President is reviewing a new Iranian proposal that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, Irans Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is in Moscow seeking Russian support amid stalled negotiations.

headlines.headline: Trump Cancels Pakistan Peace Mission, Says Iran Can Call Instead
headlines.summary: President Trump called off a planned U.S. envoy trip to Pakistan for Iran peace talks, saying Tehran can telephone if it wants to negotiate. Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi headed to Moscow as diplomatic efforts stall.

perspectives.angle: Trump Tells Iran to Call If It Wants to Negotiate as Peace Talks Stall
perspectives.summary: President Trump said Iran can reach out to the US if it wants to negotiate an end to the war, adding that US negotiators will no longer travel for talks. Both Tehran and Washington have shown little willingness to soften their terms.

headlines.headline: NATO Says No Provision to Expel Members After Pentagon Email on Spain
headlines.summary: NATO responded to reports of a Pentagon email suggesting options to suspend Spain from the alliance over perceived lack of support for Iran operations, stating there is no mechanism to expel members.

headlines.headline: Hong Kong Plans T+1 Settlement Shift to Sharpen Financial Hub Edge
headlines.summary: After more than 30 years of T+2 settlement, Hong Kong plans to move to T+1 settlement. The shift aims to strengthen Hong Kong competitive position as a global financial hub amid intensifying regional competition.

headlines.headline: US Sanctions Major Chinese Refinery Over Iranian Oil Purchases
headlines.summary: The Trump administration sanctioned Hengli Petrochemical's Dalian facility, one of China's largest independent refineries, along with 40 shipping companies for transporting Iranian oil. The move is part of a "maximum pressure" campaign to cut off Iran's key revenue source ahead of Trump's planned meeting with Xi Jinping.

perspectives.angle: US sanctions China-based oil refinery in sweeping crackdown on Iranian oil trade
perspectives.summary: Treasury targets China teapot refinery and 40 shipping entities for purchasing Iranian oil, expanding enforcement against Tehrans shadow trade network amid the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Email Floats Suspending Spain From NATO Over Iran Stance
headlines.summary: An internal Pentagon email outlines options to punish NATO allies who failed to support US operations against Iran, including suspending Spain and reviewing the US position on Britain's Falkland Islands claim. NATO officials say Trump cannot unilaterally expel members.

perspectives.angle: NATO pushes back at reported US threat to suspend Spain over Iran war support
perspectives.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlined options to punish allies perceived as lacking support for the Iran war, including suspending Spain from NATO. European allies strongly rejected the notion.

headlines.headline: China's Hengli Petrochemical Denies Trading With Iran After US Sanctions
headlines.summary: Hengli Petrochemical, one of China's largest independent refiners, denied allegations from the US Treasury that it purchased Iranian oil after Washington imposed sanctions on its Dalian subsidiary. The company insists it has never traded with Iran.

headlines.headline: China unveils worlds first coal fuel cell with zero emissions
headlines.summary: Chinese scientists developed a coal fuel cell that produces electricity with zero carbon emissions, achieving higher energy efficiency than traditional burning while eliminating pollution.

headlines.headline: US lawmaker proposes bipartisan commission on political violence after WHCA shooting
headlines.summary: Rep. Ro Khanna urged creation of a bipartisan national commission to address political violence following the shooting at the White House Correspondents Dinner.

headlines.headline: Italy to extradite Chinese hacker wanted by US authorities
headlines.summary: Italy has decided to extradite a Chinese national wanted by the US for hacking charges including stealing COVID-19 medical research, marking a significant win for US cybercrime enforcement.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo condemns warmongers as "thieves" on Chornobyl anniversary
headlines.summary: On the 40th anniversary of the Chornobyl disaster, Pope Leo issued a stark warning about nuclear power and condemned those who wage war, calling them thieves who steal humanity's peaceful future.

headlines.headline: Americans oppose ending birthright citizenship, Reuters/Ipsos poll finds
headlines.summary: A majority of Americans believe all babies born in the US should automatically receive citizenship, as the Supreme Court prepares to rule on the contentious issue.

headlines.headline: Russian Defence Minister Visits North Korea in Second High-Level Trip
headlines.summary: Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov arrived in North Korea for talks, marking the second senior Russian official to visit Pyongyang in recent days. The visit underscores deepening military ties between Moscow and Pyongyang amid the Ukraine war.

perspectives.angle: North Korea accelerates nuclear program with Russian support as Iran war erodes global security
perspectives.summary: Senior officials from Moscow and Pyongyang signal plans for long-term cooperation as North Korea capitalizes on the distraction of the Iran war to advance its nuclear ambitions, raising concerns about eroding global non-proliferation norms.

headlines.headline: Trump Says He Holds Regular Talks With Putin and Zelenskiy on Ukraine War
headlines.summary: US President Donald Trump revealed he maintains direct communication with both Russian and Ukrainian leaders as he pushes for a settlement to the ongoing conflict. The disclosure comes amid intensified diplomatic efforts.

perspectives.angle: Trump Confirms Separate Calls with Putin and Zelensky on Ukraine
perspectives.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said he has had "good conversations" with both Russian President Vladimir Putin and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky as de-escalation efforts continue.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Marks 40th Anniversary of Chornobyl Disaster Under War Clouds
headlines.summary: Ukraine commemorated four decades since the world's worst nuclear accident at Chornobyl, with officials warning that Russia's ongoing invasion could trigger another nuclear catastrophe.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Commemorates 40th Anniversary of Chornobyl Disaster Under Shadow of War
perspectives.summary: Ukraine marked the 40th anniversary of the Chornobyl nuclear disaster on Sunday amid ongoing fears that Russia's war could trigger another nuclear catastrophe. The Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant remains a concern.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine marks Chornobyl 40th anniversary under shadow of war
perspectives.summary: Ukraine commemorated the 40th anniversary of the Chornobyl nuclear disaster amid fears that Russia four-year-old war could trigger another nuclear catastrophe.

headlines.headline: China Yuan Settlements Hit Record High Amid Iran Conflict
headlines.summary: China Cross-border Interbank Payment System (CIPS) reached a record single-day transaction value of 1.22 trillion yuan, with analysts saying the Middle East conflict has acted as a catalyst for rising demand in yuan settlement, particularly in oil trade.

headlines.headline: Germany Shifts Military Strategy Away From US Dependence
headlines.summary: Germany new military doctrine emphasizes "less America, more Europe" as Berlin prepares for reduced US contributions to European security and takes greater responsibility for its own defense capabilities.

perspectives.angle: Germany Unveils First-Ever Military Strategy, Names Russia Top Threat
perspectives.summary: The Bundeswehr strategy marks Germany's first comprehensive military doctrine since WWII, explicitly identifying Russia as the primary security threat amid shifting European defense posture.

headlines.headline: Two Israeli Soldiers Killed in Jordan Border Crossing Attack
headlines.summary: Two Israeli soldiers were shot dead by a Jordanian aid truck driver near the Allenby (King Hussein) Crossing between the West Bank and Jordan. The attacker was neutralized at the site.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu orders army to attack Hezbollah in Lebanon
headlines.summary: The Israeli PM directed the military to "vigorously attack" Hezbollah in Lebanon after six people were killed by strikes despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu accuses Hezbollah of sabotaging Lebanon peace talks
perspectives.summary: Israeli PM says the Iran-backed group is actively undermining diplomatic efforts to reach a ceasefire agreement.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu warns Hezbollah is dismantling Lebanon ceasefire
perspectives.summary: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu accused Hezbollah of undermining the ceasefire agreement with Lebanon, asserting Israel will continue operations against threats. IDF struck Hezbollah operatives and infrastructure in south Lebanon.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu accuses Hezbollah of dismantling Lebanon ceasefire
perspectives.summary: Israeli PM says Hezbollah actions are threatening the ceasefire with Lebanon as sirens warn of suspected drone attacks in northern Israel.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu Orders Vigorous Attack on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Extension
perspectives.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu directed the military to "vigorously attack" Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, just two days after a ceasefire was extended. At least six people were killed in Israeli strikes on Saturday, with cross-border exchanges straining the truce agreement.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu Orders IDF Strikes on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Violations
perspectives.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu ordered the IDF to "vigorously" attack Hezbollah in southern Lebanon after the group launched projectiles into northern Israel. The ceasefire extension announced by Trump appears increasingly fragile as both sides exchange fire.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu Orders Vigorous Attacks on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Strains
perspectives.summary: Israeli PM Netanyahu ordered the military to vigorously attack Hezbollah targets in Lebanon after at least six people were killed in Israeli strikes. Cross-border exchanges have strained the extended ceasefire agreement.

headlines.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash were not authorized
headlines.summary: Mexico's government stated that two US federal agents who died in a car crash were not permitted to participate in local operations. The agents reportedly worked for the CIA.

headlines.headline: Zelensky signs cooperation deals with Azerbaijan during first wartime visit
headlines.summary: Ukrainian president continues diplomatic push in the Caucasus region following similar deals with Gulf states.

perspectives.angle: Zelensky signs cooperation deals with Azerbaijan during first wartime visit
perspectives.summary: President Zelensky met with Azerbaijani President Aliyev in Baku, marking his first wartime visit to Azerbaijan and signing bilateral cooperation agreements.

perspectives.angle: Zelensky Signs Cooperation Deals During First Wartime Visit to Azerbaijan
perspectives.summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky arrived in Azerbaijan for talks with Azerbaijani President Ilham Aliyev, signing cooperation agreements in his first wartime visit to the Caucasus nation.

headlines.headline: US Southern Command creates new autonomous warfare unit
headlines.summary: New command to integrate autonomous, semi-autonomous and unmanned platforms across Latin American operations.

headlines.headline: Malian troops and Russian mercenaries withdraw from Kidal after rebel offensive
headlines.summary: Malian troops and Russian Africa Corps mercenaries have withdrawn from the northern city of Kidal following coordinated attacks by Tuareg separatists and al-Qaeda-linked JNIM militants, marking a significant shift in the Sahel conflict.

headlines.headline: France trains Ukrainian pilots on Mirage 2000-5F fighters under EU mission
headlines.summary: French Air Force pilots are training Ukrainian crews on Mirage 2000-5F aircraft within the EUMAM framework. The training includes combat operations and maintenance, marking a concrete contribution to European security.

headlines.headline: Israeli President Delays Decision on Netanyahu Pardon, Pushes for Plea Deal
headlines.summary: President Isaac Herzog said he will delay deciding on Prime Minister Netanyahus pardon request for corruption cases, urging that mediation efforts be exhausted first. The decision comes amid the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict.

perspectives.angle: Israel President Herzog seeks plea deal over pardon for Netanyahu
perspectives.summary: Israeli President Isaac Herzog has decided to shelve Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's pardon request, instead pushing for mediation that could lead to a plea deal in his corruption trial. Netanyahu faces charges in three cases.

headlines.headline: Russia warns months needed to rebalance oil market after Hormuz blockade
headlines.summary: Russian Deputy PM Alexander Novak warned of deep energy crisis as the Hormuz blockade prevents large volumes of oil from reaching global markets.

headlines.headline: China positioned to benefit from energy-driven renewables race
